title: PlayStation State of Play June 2026 | Wolverine Confirmed, Intergalactic and God of War Rumored
subtitle: Sony's hour-plus showcase airs June 2 at 5 PM ET. Insomniac's Marvel's Wolverine opens the show with its biggest gameplay reveal yet, ahead of a September 15 release. Naughty Dog's Intergalactic and a new God of War are the two rumors that could make this one of Sony's biggest showcases in years.
subject: PlayStation State of Play — June 2, 2026 Preview
description: Sony hosts a 60-plus minute State of Play on June 2, 2026 at 5 PM ET. Marvel's Wolverine gameplay confirmed. Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet debut and a new God of War are the headline rumors. Full preview of every confirmed and expected announcement.

timeline.description: Naughty Dog reveals Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet for the first time with a cinematic teaser. No gameplay shown. Jordan A. Mun and the planet Sempiria introduced. Industry files it as 2026 or 2027 at earliest.
Marvel's Wolverine shown in targeted previews at Sony events. September 15, 2026 release date confirmed. Insomniac positions it as the next flagship PS5 exclusive after the Spider-Man series.

Sony officially announces the June 2 State of Play. Runtime confirmed at over one hour. Marvel's Wolverine gameplay confirmed as part of the show. Intergalactic and God of War rumors reach peak intensity in the hours before the showcase.
PlayStation State of Play airs live at 5:00 PM ET / 2:00 PM PT. Over one hour of PS5 content. All announced and rumored titles expected to receive air time.

footer_tagline: OzoneNews | Preview based on official Sony announcements and pre-show industry reporting as of June 1, 2026.
content_html:
What to Know

Sony is hosting a PlayStation State of Play on Tuesday, June 2, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET / 2:00 PM PT. The showcase runs over one hour. Marvel's Wolverine is confirmed to open the show with its biggest gameplay look yet, ahead of a September 15 release. Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet and a new God of War are the two biggest unconfirmed appearances with strong industry backing.[1]

Insomniac Games is officially kicking off the show. Sony has confirmed that Marvel's Wolverine will receive an extended gameplay segment, the most substantial public look at the game since its initial reveal. Sony's own language promises a showcase of "Logan's brutal and relentless combat," which directly signals that the close-quarters, visceral brawling system Insomniac has been building will finally be shown at length.[2]

With a September 15, 2026 release date locked, this State of Play appearance functions as the game's primary pre-launch marketing moment. Insomniac's track record with the Spider-Man series gives the studio significant credibility heading into a completely different Marvel IP, and the gameplay reveal tomorrow will be the first real test of whether Wolverine's combat identity stands apart from what the studio built for Peter Parker and Miles Morales.

The biggest unconfirmed appearance on the show is Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet from Naughty Dog. The game was first revealed 18 months ago with a cinematic teaser introducing Jordan A. Mun, a bounty hunter stranded on a cut-off planet called Sempiria. Since that reveal, Naughty Dog and director Neil Druckmann have said almost nothing publicly, other than Druckmann describing it as the studio's "most ambitious game" ever.[3]

Industry insiders are pointing heavily toward a gameplay debut tomorrow. If accurate, this would be the show's defining moment: Naughty Dog's first new IP since The Last of Us, from the director who shaped modern PlayStation narrative gaming, getting its first real gameplay reveal after a year and a half of silence. The rumor has enough sourcing behind it that its absence would be the more surprising outcome.

Santa Monica Studio's next project is the showcase's second major rumor. Two distinct scenarios are circulating among industry sources: a God of War spin-off focused on Kratos' wife, Faye, exploring the events the main trilogy referenced but never depicted, or an entirely new IP directed by Cory Barlog, the architect of the 2018 God of War reimagining. Either would represent a significant announcement.

The Faye spin-off rumor has the more specific sourcing, but Barlog's public comments over the past year have consistently gestured toward something new rather than a continuation of the Kratos arc. Both possibilities are credible. If either appears tomorrow, it becomes the show's second headline alongside Intergalactic.
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls — A new PS5 fighting game with an August launch window, expected to receive a full trailer and release date confirmation.
  • Final Fantasy VII Remake Part 3 — Fan speculation is high, though no insider sourcing has firmly placed this at the show. A teaser would be a significant surprise rather than an expectation.
  • PSVR2 and Hardware Accessories — Whispers of potential PSVR2 software news or new peripheral announcements have circulated ahead of the showcase.

This shapes up as one of Sony's most substantive State of Play presentations in several years. A confirmed Wolverine gameplay reveal plus potential debuts from Naughty Dog and Santa Monica Studio in the same hour would represent a significant concentration of first-party firepower. Follow the OzoneNews Video Games Hub for full coverage and recap immediately after the show airs tomorrow.
